title: METHOD FOR MANUFACTURING A MICROSPHERES NETWORK
abstract: The present invention relates to a method for manufacturing a microsphere array on a substrate, the method comprising the steps of providing a dispersion containing microspheres in a liquid medium, spin-coating the dispersion onto the substrate to form the microsphere array and then subjecting the microsphere array to gravitational and vibrational forces to cause re-distribution of the microspheres on the substrate.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for manufacturing a microsphere array on a substrate, the method comprising the steps of providing a dispersion containing microspheres in a liquid medium, spin-coating the dispersion onto the substrate to form the microsphere array and then subjecting the microsphere array to gravitational and vibrational forces to cause re-distribution of the microspheres on the substrate. 2. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microspheres are subjected to the gravitational and vibrational forces simultaneously. 3. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the step of subjecting the microspheres to gravitational forces comprises adjusting the angle of the substrate to form a sloping substrate. 4. Method according to claim 3, wherein the angle of the sloping substrate is at least 5Â°. 5. Method according to claim 3 or claim 4, wherein the angle of the sloping substrate is between 10Â° and 75Â°. 6. Method according to any one of claims 3 to 5, wherein angle of the sloping substrate is between 20Â° and 60Â°. 7. Method according to any one of claims 3 to 6, wherein angle of the sloping substrate is between 45Â° and 60Â°. 8. Method according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein the step of adjusting the angle of the substrate comprises placing the substrate on a slope. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microspheres are subjected to a vibration treatment of at least 10 KHz. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the wherein the microspheres are subjected to a vibration treatment of 10-100 KHz. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the wherein the microspheres are subjected to a vibration treatment of 20-60 KHz. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microsphere array is subjected to a centrifugal force. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microspheres have a diameter of less than 100 Î¼Î¹Î·. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microspheres have a diameter of 2-30 Î¼Î¹Î·. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microspheres have a diameter of 5-10 Î¼Î¹Î·. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microspheres comprise Barium Titanate (BaTiC ), polystyrene, diamond, sapphire (AI2O3), titanium dioxide, cubic zirconia, zinc oxide, silicon, germanium, gallium phosphide or gallium arsenide. Method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microspheres comprise silica (S1O2). An objective lens attachment for a microscope, the attachment comprising: a cap attachable to an outer housing of the objective lens; a support body affixed 

 to the cap; an adhesive layer provided on the support body and a microsphere lens affixed to the support body by the adhesive layer, wherein the microsphere lens comprises microspheres obtained in accordance with the method of any one of claims 1 to 17. An objective lens attachment according to claim 18, wherein the adhesive layer comprises an optically clear double-sided tape. An objective lens attachment according to claim 18 or claim 19, wherein the adhesive layer may have a thickness in the range 30 -150 Î¼Î¹Î·. An objective lens attachment according to any of claims 18 to 20, wherein microsphere lens has a width dimension in the range of 30-1000 Î¼Î¹Î·. A method of constructing an attachment for an objective lens, the method comprising the steps of affixing a support body to a cap attachable to an outer housing of an objective lens; providing an adhesive layer on the support body, and affixing microspheres produced according to the method of any one of claims 1 to 17 to the adhesive layer. Method according to claim 22, wherein the microspheres are affixed to the adhesive layer after the step subjecting the microsphere array to gravitational and vibrational forces. Method according to claim 22 or claim 23, wherein a mask having a predetermined shape is provided on the substrate to define one or more exposed substrate regions and one or more non-exposed substrate regions. 25. Method according to any one of claims 22 to 24, wherein the microspheres are affixed to the adhesive layer by bringing a substrate facing side of the adhesive layer into contact with the microspheres. 26. Use of the microspheres obtained in accordance with the method of claims 1 to 17 in the manufacture of a microsphere lens for an objective lens attachment for a microscope.
